messages.content: Hi. I want to downgrade my plan. I've been thinking about it and I want to move to the Basic plan.

Hi Priya — I can process that, but before I do I want to make sure you have the full picture, especially given your account history. You're on Pro Annual, and I have notes from your last session about the client portal Gantt gap affecting your renewal decision, and a customer success manager was supposed to reach out. Did that conversation happen? I ask because a downgrade to Basic would remove several features your team is actively using — client portal access, advanced reporting, and more. I want to make sure this is the right move for you.

The customer success person did call me. She told me the client portal Gantt is at least 6 months away. I was hoping it would be sooner. And honestly the price is also a factor right now — we're tightening budgets across the board this quarter.

That's completely understandable — I appreciate you explaining the context. Before I process the downgrade, let me be transparent about what you'd lose on Basic: team size limit drops to 5 seats (you currently have 10, with 13 planned), client portal access disappears entirely, Gantt view gone, and integrations including Slack become limited. Given that you have 10 people on the account right now, Basic wouldn't actually support your team at all. The only plan that covers 10+ people is Pro or Business. What's driving the decision more — the feature gap or the price? There may be options.
Oh. I didn't realize Basic only goes to 5 seats. That's a problem. I can't downgrade then without splitting my team somehow, which isn't feasible.

Right — Basic at 5 seats would effectively mean half your team loses access immediately. That's clearly not workable. So realistically your options are: stay on Pro Annual as you are, or upgrade to Business which has unlimited seats and includes client portal Gantt on the roadmap as a higher-priority feature. Given the budget context, I suspect staying on Pro is the right call for now. I can also look at whether there are any loyalty adjustments we can offer given the product gap that's affecting your renewal sentiment. Would that be useful?
Yes, a discount would definitely help. What can you do?

Given the documented feature gap that's impacting your primary use case and the length of time it's taken to resolve the Slack integration issue, I'm authorized to offer a 15% discount on your renewal when it comes due. I'll note this formally on your account so it's locked in regardless of who processes your renewal — you don't need to negotiate again. I'm also flagging the budget sensitivity and the downgrade inquiry as additional renewal risk indicators. I want to be honest: a 15% discount isn't a substitute for the feature you need, but it's a concrete acknowledgment of the gap while you wait on the roadmap. Does that feel fair?
That's reasonable. Yes, let's keep Pro for now and apply that discount at renewal. Let's not do the downgrade.

Perfect — downgrade cancelled, discount locked at 15% on renewal. I've updated your account status from "downgrade requested" back to "active Pro Annual, renewal discount confirmed." I'm also noting this session so the full context — budget pressure, downgrade consideration, feature gap, CS conversation, discount applied — is all in one place for anyone who works with your account going forward. Is the Slack issue resolved by the way? I escalated it as a regression in our last session.
Actually no — the Slack thing is still happening intermittently. It's better than it was but not fully fixed. Some days we get the triple notifications, some days we don't.

That's not acceptable given how long this has been open. I'm marking the Slack ticket as unresolved and re-escalating with an urgent flag — intermittent behavior after a supposed fix often means the root cause wasn't fully addressed. I'm attaching your account to the bug ticket directly so engineering can see the real-world impact. I'll personally follow up on this within 3 business days with a status update rather than waiting for you to come back to us.
